preamble: The transformation of a voiceless uvular fricative [χ] into a voiceless uvular stop [q] represents a fortition process. This is often found in emphatic registers or as dialectal variation within languages like Arabic or Turkic, where the fricative manner of articulation is replaced by a complete closure.

examples.note: Emphatic or dialectal strengthening of uvular fricatives.
Historical variation in the realization of back-series stops/fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
